parsed_review.summary: CP00-274 is a descriptor-only RP08 email-dms-core model-binding slice pack (40 units, RP08.P01.M04.S07-RP08.P01.M06.S04, risk B). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level production_ready claim is supported and properly gated: command-evidence currently records production_ready=false with status implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout, and the manifest pack_level_claude_review.status=pending with empty valid_review_receipts, so nothing is over-claimed ahead of this review. Coverage is exact: registry CP274 requirements (M04 tail 14 rows + M05 full 22-row permission/audit cycle + M06 head 4 rows) and deliverable distribution (impl 22, ui 6, fixture 2, test 6, hermes 2, claude 2) and micro-title distribution (Secondary Workflow Slice 14, Permission And Audit Binding 22, Synthetic Fixture Set 4) match the prompt baseline, the plan-of-record pack, the contract historical binding tail, and the unit test assertions. The descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundary holds throughout: service.js freezes results with all runtime flags false (no email/office/sync/filing dispatch, no DB rows, no object storage, no audit write, no state transition, no Hermes runtime receipt, no command runtime), validators.js enforces descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/raw_payload_excluded on every row, and validator/test code only reads files. Authority boundary is intact: claude_is_final_approval=false, human final approval required for runtime opening, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, and Hermes patterns used only for deterministic descriptor packet/receipt handling. Review-harness hardening is satisfied: tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob (forbidden_tools_present=false), prompt 2236B under the 25600B cap with no embedded full diff/source, auth preflight passed with well-formed non-empty JSON (not 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped), and summary-only review correctly prohibited for a risk-B pack. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer, not final approver. One non-blocking P3: changed-file-scope.json classifies the untracked handoff doc inconsistently versus the manifest, with no impact on code, boundaries, or the production_ready claim. No P0/P1/P2 findings.

findings.title: Untracked handoff doc classified inconsistently across scope metadata
findings.details: changed-file-scope.json lists docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md under both untracked_implementation_files (line 96) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 102), while the manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json lines 1689-1692) enumerates only the .docx and the LDIP c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json and omits new-session-handoff.md. This is a cosmetic inconsistency in untracked-file scoping metadata for a documentation handoff file. It does not affect pack source, the descriptor-only/no-write boundaries, coverage, or the production_ready claim, and active_pack_required_untracked_files is empty so no required artifact is missing.
findings.recommendation: Align the untracked-file classification between changed-file-scope.json and the manifest (treat new-session-handoff.md consistently as either an unrelated preserved file or an out-of-scope doc) for cleaner scope reporting. No code or boundary change required; non-blocking for CP00-274 closeout.
